description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

Today a subject's binding health is only inferable by clicking into Coverage and reading the bindings <dl> (lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex:1132-1150). Surface it on the subject card header (subject_template, html.ex:628-664) alongside the existing change/finding badges:

- "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)"
- "N bindings, K dangling"
- "N bindings, all valid"

Pulls from subject.bindings + the dangling-binding finding count for that subject. This makes binding state visible during triage instead of forcing a tab-dive.

- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex (subject_template + new render_subject_binding_health/2 + tooltip variants + CSS rules)
- test/specled_ex/review_test.exs (new describe blocks for the helper and the integration)

Tests added:
- Html.render_subject_binding_health/2 — 7 unit tests (empty/nil bindings, all-valid plural+singular, dangling count, ignores non-dangling codes, single MFA value)
- subject card binding-health badge integration — 1 end-to-end test through Review.build_view + Html.render

Verification stubs flipped: none (the existing executed `mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs ...` stub already covers specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges; the new tests are tagged `spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"` so they're picked up by tag-based coverage).

Summary: subject_template now renders a third header chip via render_subject_binding_health/2, alongside the existing change and finding badges. The helper sums MFAs across realized_by tiers (List.wrap parity with render_bindings_section) and counts branch_guard_dangling_binding findings on the subject to pick one of three states: "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)", "N bindings, K dangling", or "N bindings, all valid". Adds matching CSS (badge-binding-empty/dangling/valid) and tooltip text for each state. mix spec.check stays clean; full test suite (572 tests + 2 properties) green.

Note on context isolation: this Claude Code environment exposes no Task/Agent spawn tool, so the cold-context verifier could not be spawned. Verification was performed mechanically and inline by the implementer in the same session. The mechanical checks below are independent of the implementation narrative; the auditor (or orchestrator review) should weight that accordingly.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs)

- mix.exs unchanged (no new deps); no refactors; only additions.

Semantic checks:
- Spec verification stub `mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs` — 19 tests, 0 failures.
- The ticket advances specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges. The new badge attaches to the subject card header and reuses the same per-subject `branch_guard_dangling_binding` finding stream; integration test confirms `badge-binding-health` appears in rendered HTML.
- Three states render as specified by the ticket body:
  * "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)" — empty/nil bindings
  * "N bindings, K dangling" — dangling count from findings
  * "N bindings, all valid" — bindings present, no dangling
- Plural/singular handled (`maybe_plural`); non-list MFA values handled via `List.wrap` parity with render_bindings_section.
- mix spec.check: status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, findings=0.
- Full test suite: 572 tests + 2 properties, 0 failures.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Spec did not need a new requirement; the ticket explicitly extends the existing inline_finding_badges contract by surfacing a derived per-subject view.
- Provenance trace: dangling count pulls from `f["code"] == "branch_guard_dangling_binding"`, which matches the canonical code defined in lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:21 and the per-tier modules (api_boundary, implementation, expanded_behavior, typespecs, use). Findings are grouped by subject_id in Review.group_findings_by_subject/1 and threaded into `s.findings` in build_subject_view/7, so the count is sourced from the same canonical stream the triage panel uses.
- Source-claim trace for `s.bindings`: populated at lib/specled_ex/review.ex:238 from `meta_field(meta, :realized_by, %{})`. The new helper iterates `Map.values/1` and List.wraps each — same shape contract as the existing render_bindings_section at html.ex:1132-1150.

title: Per-subject binding-health callout on subject card header
